---
description: Smart upsert local work items / test cases / wiki / pipeline-triggers into ADO or JIRA
agent: alm
phase: ALM

---

# /alm push

> Smart upsert by default. Matches local artefacts to ALM by stable id (in `traceability.yaml`); falls back to title search within the parent suite when no ALM id is yet recorded. Conflict-detected via hash + last-synced timestamp.

## Usage

```
/alm push <artifact> [--create-only|--update-only] [--strategy merge|overwrite|fail-on-conflict] [--dry-run] [--roundtrip-check] [--feature <f>] [--suite <slug>] [--project <name>]
```

`<artifact>`: `work-items` | `tests` | `wiki` | `pipeline-trigger`

## Flags

| Flag | Default | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `--strategy merge` | yes | Local-changed fields overwrite ALM; ALM-changed fields not declared locally preserved |
| `--strategy overwrite` | no | Replace ALM state entirely; unspecified fields cleared |
| `--strategy fail-on-conflict` | no | Abort if ALM was edited since last pull (diff report shown) |
| `--create-only` | no | Fail if any local artefact already exists in ALM (first-time publication) |
| `--update-only` | no | Fail if any local artefact does NOT exist in ALM (refresh existing only) |
| `--dry-run` | no | Preview with per-item create/update diff; **no ALM writes**; runs the push-review checklist inline |
| `--roundtrip-check` | depends on `project.config.yaml alm.roundtrip-check` | Warn before push if content is not round-trippable |
| `--feature <f>` | none | Scope push to one feature (required for `--suite`) |
| `--suite <slug>` | none | Scope push to one test suite — incremental |
| `--project <name>` | resolved from cwd | Project selector |

## Inputs

- `project.config.yaml` — `alm.tool` + adapter config
- `projects/{p}/work-items.yaml` — local L1-L4 ledger (validated against [`schemas/work-items.v1.json`](../../schemas/work-items.v1.json))
- `projects/{p}/{agent}/features/{f}/traceability.yaml` — per-feature ALM id + hash + last-synced timestamp
- `projects/{p}/_handoffs/*-alm.handoff.json` — when present, processed as pending pushes (after validation against `schemas/handoff.v1.json` + `schemas/alm-extract.v1.json`)
- [`templates/alm-push-report.template.md`](../../templates/alm-push-report.template.md)

## Execution flow

1. Resolve project + `alm.tool` (ADO or JIRA) + adapter config.
2. Load `work-items.yaml` partition scoped by `--feature` / `--suite` / `--agent` filters.
3. For each `_handoffs/*-alm.handoff.json` not yet processed:
   - Validate against `schemas/handoff.v1.json`; payload against `schemas/alm-extract.v1.json`
   - Merge into the `work-items.yaml` working set
4. For each work item in the working set:
   - Resolve ALM id from `traceability.yaml`
   - Fetch current ALM state (MCP `alm_get_work_item`)
   - Compute current ALM hash vs `pulledHash` in traceability
   - On `--strategy fail-on-conflict`: abort if hashes differ
   - On `--strategy merge`: produce field-level diff merging local + ALM
   - On `--strategy overwrite`: produce field-level diff replacing ALM
5. For each test case in `<artifact>=tests`:
   - Use `alm_add_steps` converter to produce ADO Steps XML or JIRA test-tool fields
6. For markdown content fields (description, AC, comments):
   - Convert via `alm_convert_md_to_alm({md, target: ado|jira-cloud|jira-server, field})`
   - Render Mermaid via `alm_render_mermaid`; upload as attachment; embed `<img>` + preserve source as `<pre><code class="mermaid">`
   - When `--roundtrip-check`: run `alm_roundtrip_check`; warn on non-round-trippable
7. On `--dry-run`:
   - Render `alm-push-report.template.md` showing planned creates / updates / conflicts; **no writes**
   - Run [`templates/checklists/alm-push-review.checklist.md`](../../templates/checklists/alm-push-review.checklist.md) inline
   - Print verdict; exit 0
8. Otherwise execute changes:
   - Bulk-create via `alm_bulk_create_work_items` (≤200 per batch)
   - Per-item updates via `alm_update_work_item`
   - Test case + steps via `alm_create_test_case` + `alm_add_steps`
   - Update `traceability.yaml` with `almId`, `lastPushedAt`, `pushedHash`, `pulledHash` (immediate round-trip read)
   - Write `traceability_write_alm_ids` (MCP) back to `work-items.yaml`
9. Render `projects/{p}/alm-reports/push-{ts}.md`.

## Output

- `projects/{p}/alm-reports/push-{ts}.md` — full report
- `projects/{p}/work-items.yaml` — updated `almId` fields
- `projects/{p}/{agent}/features/{f}/traceability.yaml` — updated hashes + timestamps
- (When real run) ALM work items + test cases + (when in scope) wiki pages + pipeline triggers

## ID strategy

ALM ids stored in `traceability.yaml` per feature; the `localUID` is the local stable id (per [design/08-traceability.md](../../../design/08-traceability.md)). Stable id format examples:
- CE: `ce-{feature-slug}-{tier}-NN`
- FO: `fo-{feature-slug}-{tier}-NN`
- INT: `int-{feature-slug}-{tier}-NN`
- REP: `rep-{feature-slug}-{tier}-NN`
